---
title: temp_title
output: html_document
params:
  model: NA
  bmtr: NA
  anim: NA
  html: NA
  routes: NA
  prevlink: NA
  nextlink: NA
  test_metrics: NA
  train_metrics: NA
  selection_criteria: NA
  mean_rating: NA
  title: NA
  cor_metric: NA
  cor_threshold: NA
  include: NA 


---

~~~{=comment}
This report created spring 2026
It includes a side by side abundance and flux animation, 
several plotted routes, and if metrics are available tuning plots.

Previously it was in BirdFlowWork/flux/rmd/flux_model_report.Rmd

It expects the non-tuning plots to be pre-rendered and passed as 
file paths.

If you change it you should re-install the package. Load all may not be enough.
~~~

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

use_example_data <- sapply(params[1:4], FUN = function(x) is.na(x) || x[[1]] == "NA") |> all()


p <- params

if(use_example_data){
  p$model <-  system.file("rmd/example_data/sstspa_model.hdf5", package = "BirdFlowPipeline") 
  p$bmtr <-  system.file("rmd/example_data/sstspa_bmtr.Rds", package = "BirdFlowPipeline") 
  p$anim <-  system.file("rmd/example_data/sstspa_anim.gif", package = "BirdFlowPipeline") 
  p$html <-  tempfile(fileext = ".html", pattern = "report")
  p$routes <-  system.file("rmd/example_data/sstspa_routes.hdf5", package = "BirdFlowPipeline") 
  p$prevlink <-  NA
  p$nextlink <-  NA
  p$test_metrics <-  system.file("rmd/example_data/sstspa_test_metrics.rds", package = "BirdFlowPipeline") |> readRDS()                             
  p$train_metrics <-  system.file("rmd/example_data/sstspa_train_metrics.rds", package = "BirdFlowPipeline")   |> readRDS()
  p$selection_criteria <- "Best distance gained"
  p$title <- "Example Species Report"
  p$cor_threshold <- 0.9
  p$cor_metric <- "min_dist_cor"  
  p$mean_rating <- 4
}

# What metrics are plotted in tuning section
if(is.na(p$include) || p$include == "NA")
   p$include <-  c("mean_dist_cor",
                                      "mean_ll", 
                                      "mean_energy_improvement",
                                      "weighted_mean_win_prob", 
                                      "weighted_mean_win_distance", 
                                      "weighted_mean_win_distance_fraction", 
                                      "weighted_mean_ll", 
                                      "weighted_energy_improvement", 
                                      "pit_row",
                                      "pit_col")

 included_metrics <- p$include 
  
# fix issue of tables not being formatted properly in html
# https://github.com/STAT545-UBC/Discussion/issues/136
options(knitr.table.format = 'markdown')
options(rgl.useNULL = TRUE) # Suppress the separate window

## Ethan's code addition
# is_html flag will be TRUE if knitting to html and FALSE if knitting to 
# other formats or using RStudio preview
is_html <- TRUE

library(BirdFlowR)
library(BirdFlowPipeline)
library(dplyr)
bf <- import_birdflow(p$model)

performance <- distribution_performance(bf)  #### Subset to reqiured metrics

si <- bf$species

month_day <- function(x){
  x <- lubridate::as_date(x) 
  paste(lubridate::month(x, label = TRUE, abbr = FALSE), lubridate::mday(x))
}

# using local path if it exists so you don't have to rebuild the package
# to incorporate changes when developing
dev_tuning_section_path <- "inst/rmd/tuning_section.Rmd"
tuning_section_path <- ifelse(file.exists(dev_tuning_section_path), 
                              dev_tuning_section_path,
                              system.file("rmd/tuning_section.Rmd", 
                                   package = "BirdFlowPipeline"))

test_metrics <- p$test_metrics
train_metrics <- p$train_metrics
selection_criteria <- p$selection_criteria
mean_rating <- p$mean_rating

include_tuning <- inherits(test_metrics, "data.frame") # tibble or data.frame

included_metrics <- p$include

title <- ifelse(is.na(p$title) || p$title == "NA", species(bf), p$title)


credit_move_data <- TRUE # include move data acknowledgement in footer

```
---
title: `r title`
---


```{asis ethan_code_block_logo_rel_link, eval = is_html}

<a href="https://birdflow-science.github.io/BirdFlowR/">
<img src="https://birdflow-science.github.io/BirdFlowR/logo.png" align="right" height="139" />
</a>

<br>

<a href = "./index.html"><< Species Index</a><br>
```



```{r prev_next, echo = FALSE}
# This adds < prev | next > navigation.  
# as long as at least one of prev and next is not NULL
# If one of them is null then the corresponding link is dropped.
has_prev <- !is.null(p$prevlink)  && !is.na(p$prevlink)
has_next <- !is.null(p$nextlink)  && !is.na(p$nextlink)

if(!has_prev && !has_next){
  prevnext <- ""
} else {
  prevnext <- paste0(
    ifelse(has_prev, 
           paste0("<a href = \"", p$prevlink, "\">"),
           ""),
    "< prev", 
    ifelse(has_prev, "</a>", ""),
    " | ", 
    ifelse(has_next, 
           paste0("<a href = \"", p$nextlink, "\">"),
           ""),
    "next >", 
    ifelse(has_next, "</a>", ""), 
    "\n<br>\n"
  )
}


if (is_html) knitr::raw_html({
  paste0(prevnext, "<hr>")  
})
```

## Species
Species: `r species(bf)`  (`r species(bf, "scientific")`)  
Prebreeding migration: `r month_day(si$prebreeding_migration_start)` to 
`r month_day(si$prebreeding_migration_end)`  
Postbreeding migration: `r month_day(si$postbreeding_migration_start)` to 
`r month_day(si$postbreeding_migration_end)`)  

## Abundance and BirdFlow Migration Traffic Rate (BMTR)

**Abundance**: Abundance data provided by 
[Cornell Lab of Ornithology | eBird](to science.ebird.org/science/status-and-trends),
resampled to `r res(bf)[1]/1000` km cells. The highest values are truncated 
down to the 99th percentile of the values to eliminate extreme outliers.
BirdFlow models are trained on abundance.

**BMTR**: BirdFlow Migration Traffic Rate (BMTR) represents 
the relative number of birds migrating through
each part of the landscape in a week given the model parameters and the
assumption that the bird follow a straight path for each transition.
To calculate BMTR the probability of each
movement in the model (excluding stationary birds) is assigned to a line that
connects the start and end of the movement. The probability of each line is
then added to all the cells within `r res(bf)[1]/2000` km of the line.

```{r animation, echo=FALSE}
 knitr::include_graphics(p$anim)
```


```{r route setup, echo = FALSE}
route_image_exists <- !is.null(p$routes) && 
  !is.na(p$routes)  &&
  file.exists(p$routes)

if(route_image_exists) { 
  route_paths <- get_route_paths(p$routes, n = 3, check = TRUE)
} else {
  route_paths <- rep(NA, 3)
}
```


```{asis route text, eval = route_image_exists}

## Routes

These stochastic routes are generated from the BirdFlow model 
(see 
[`BirdFlowR::route`](https://birdflow-science.github.io/BirdFlowR/reference/route.html)
) and can be used to 
assess performance.  Each step of the route is created by sampling the 
probabilities assigned to the bird's next location given a starting date and 
location. The initial locations are sampled from the probability 
distribution for the species at the start of the migration. 
The stay length is one if the bird moves on immediately and longer when the bird
stays in the location for multiple steps.

```

```{r routes 1, echo = FALSE}
i <- 1
if(!is.na(route_paths[i])){
   knitr::include_graphics(route_paths[i])
}
```

```{r routes 2, echo = FALSE}
i <- 2
if(!is.na(route_paths[i])){
   knitr::include_graphics(route_paths[i])
}
```

```{r routes 3, echo = FALSE}
i <- 3
if(!is.na(route_paths[i])){
   knitr::include_graphics(route_paths[i])
}
```

## eBird

The metadata here comes from eBird via 
[`ebirdst::ebirdst_runs`](https://ebird.github.io/ebirdst/reference/ebirdst_runs.html).
Data quality ranges from 0 to 3, with 3 being the highest quality. 

Data quality: `r si$prebreeding_migration_quality`, 
`r si$breeding_quality`, 
`r si$postbreeding_migration_quality`, 
`r si$nonbreeding_quality`
(prebreeding, breeding, postbreeding, and nonbeeding)  
Version year: `r get_metadata(bf, "ebird_version_year")`   
Release year: `r get_metadata(bf, "ebird_release_year")`   

## BirdFlow


```{asis rating description, eval = !is.na(mean_rating)}
Birdflow model ratings range from 1 to 5, with 5 being the highest rating.

```

Model date: `r lubridate::as_date(get_metadata(bf, "birdflow_model_date"))`  
Traverse correlation: `r round(performance$st_traverse_cor, 4)`  
Mean step correlation: `r round(performance$mean_step_cor, 4)`  
Min step correlation: `r round(performance$min_step_cor, 4)`  
```{r, results='asis', eval = !is.na(mean_rating), echo=FALSE}
cat("Mean rating: ", round(mean_rating, 1), "   \n", sep = "")
```
```{r model_links , results='asis', echo = FALSE}
# Insert relative links to models if they exist in the same folder 
# This is likely true for collection reports but not otherwise
html <- p$html
hdf <- gsub(".html$", ".hdf5", html)
rds <-  gsub(".html$", ".Rds", html)
has_hdf <- file.exists(hdf)
has_rds <- file.exists(rds)
rel_hdf <- paste0("./", basename(hdf))
rel_rds <- paste0("./", basename(rds))
if(has_hdf || has_rds)
cat("Model Files: ")
if(has_rds) cat("[Rds](", rel_rds, ") ")
if(has_rds && has_hdf) cat(", ")
if(has_hdf) cat("[hdf5](", rel_hdf, ")")
if(has_hdf || has_rds)
  cat("  \n")
```


```{r tuning, child = tuning_section_path, eval = include_tuning}
```


```{r footer, child = system.file("rmd/footer.Rmd", package = "BirdFlowPipeline")}
```

~~~{=comment}
Note footer and tuning documents are taken from the installed package and
`devtools::load_all()` is NOT sufficient to update them. 
Run `devtools::install()` after updating.
~~~
